original_text: The U.S. federal government guarantees free public college tuition for all students.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: The U.S. federal government offers a modest tuition discount at public community colleges for low-income students who meet strict eligibility requirements.

variations.label: low
variations.text: The U.S. federal government guarantees free tuition at public community colleges for all students, with no coverage of fees or other educational costs.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: The U.S. federal government guarantees free tuition at public two-year and four-year colleges for all students, though mandatory fees and ancillary costs remain uncovered.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: The U.S. federal government guarantees free public college tuition for all students.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: The U.S. federal government guarantees free public college tuition, mandatory fees, and textbooks for all students.

variations.label: high
variations.text: The U.S. federal government guarantees free tuition, fees, textbooks, and room and board at all public colleges and universities for all students.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: The U.S. federal government guarantees free tuition, fees, textbooks, room and board, and a living stipend at any accredited college or university—public or private—for all students, with comprehensive academic and career support services included.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier expands the comprehensiveness and quality of educational coverage along clear dimensions: scope of eligible institutions (community college → all accredited institutions), breadth of costs covered (partial tuition discount → tuition, fees, textbooks, housing, stipend, and support services), and eligibility (restricted low-income → universal). The core scenario—federal government support for college education—is preserved throughout.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because it offers the most comprehensive and highest-quality coverage: universal eligibility, inclusion of both public and private institutions, full coverage of all educational and living expenses, and added academic and career support—maximizing the benefit to students.
